is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated malaria caused by Plasmodium falciparum; genotyped pfdhfr and pfdhps genes.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Genotyped pfdhfr residues 51,59,108,164 and pfdhps residues 436,437,540,581,613 — including curated targets Pfdhfr 51,59,108 and Pfdhps 436,437,540,581,613.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Figs 1-2 give per-site genotype frequencies; text gives codon-specific prevalences (e.g. codon 437 Gly 97% Rukara, 80% Mashesha; codon 581 Gly 60% Rukara, 29% Mashesha; 164-Leu 11.4% Rukara). Denominators 725 total; Tables 1-2 give counts.

sub_country_location.evidence: All frequencies reported for two named sites: Rukara (eastern province) and Mashesha/Muganza (western province), below country level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples from the 2006 CPG-DDS+A vs AQ+SP trial (NCT00461578); collection bounded to 2006, within 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children with uncomplicated malaria collected at two sites in Rwanda.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical genotyping study of trial isolates with original frequency data.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples derive from a previously published randomised trial (Fanello et al. 2008, NCT00461578); molecular data may overlap with that trial's clinical dataset, though the marker frequencies appear reported here first.

supplement.evidence: Per-locus frequencies and site-level breakdowns are given in main text figures and tables; no supplementary pointer for extraction.

assessment.summary: Molecular study of pfdhfr and pfdhps antifolate-resistance mutations in 725 P. falciparum isolates from Rwandan children at two sites (Rukara, eastern; Mashesha/Muganza, western) during a 2006 drug trial. Reports site-level codon frequencies for curated markers (pfdhfr 51,59,108; pfdhps 436,437,540,581,613), meeting all eligibility criteria.
